matching_end_count: The top chain ends with a tile marked `REF`. {connection_rule_text} How many loose dominoes below can connect to that open right end?
Focus on the tile marked `REF` at the end of the top chain. {connection_rule_text} Count the loose dominoes below that could connect to its open right end.
The chain's last tile is marked `REF`. {connection_rule_text} How many loose dominoes shown below match that open right end?
Use the `REF` tile at the end of the chain. {connection_rule_text} How many loose dominoes below could be attached to the open right end?
The right end of the `REF` tile is open. {connection_rule_text} How many loose dominoes below could legally connect there?
longest_chain_length_value: Starting from the open right end of `REF`. {longest_chain_rule_text} What is the maximum number of loose dominoes that can be added?
{longest_chain_rule_text} Beginning at `REF`'s open right end, what is the longest chain length using the loose dominoes?
Use the open right end of the tile marked `REF`. {longest_chain_rule_text} How many loose dominoes can the longest possible added chain contain?
From the `REF` tile's open right end. {longest_chain_rule_text} Give the maximum number of loose dominoes that can be played in sequence.
Build one chain outward from `REF`. {longest_chain_rule_text} What is the greatest number of loose dominoes that can be used?
invalid_join_label: {invalid_join_rule_text} Which labeled join breaks the chain rule?
The joins between neighboring dominoes are labeled `A` through `F`. {invalid_join_rule_text} Which join label is invalid?
{invalid_join_rule_text} Find the one labeled join where the touching halves do not match.
Look at the six labeled joins in the chain. {invalid_join_rule_text} Which label marks the broken join?
Each label marks a join between two neighboring dominoes. {invalid_join_rule_text} What is the invalid join label?
higher_sum_than_reference_count: The tile marked `REF` is the reference tile. {pip_sum_rule_text} How many other face-up dominoes have a larger pip sum than `REF`?
{pip_sum_rule_text} Count the face-up dominoes, excluding `REF`, whose pip sum is greater than the `REF` tile.
Using the tile marked `REF` as the reference, how many other face-up dominoes have a higher total? {pip_sum_rule_text}
{pip_sum_rule_text} What is the number of non-`REF` dominoes whose pip sum exceeds the `REF` tile's pip sum?
Compare the other face-up dominoes against the tile marked `REF`. {pip_sum_rule_text} How many have a larger total?
sum_to_target_count: {pip_sum_rule_text} How many face-up dominoes have pip sum equal to {target_total_text}?
Count the face-up dominoes whose pip sum is {target_total_text}. {pip_sum_rule_text}
{pip_sum_rule_text} What is the number of shown dominoes with total {target_total_text}?
Looking only at the shown dominoes, {pip_sum_rule_text} how many have pip sum {target_total_text}?
{pip_sum_rule_text} Determine how many face-up dominoes add up to {target_total_text}.
double_count: {double_rule_text} How many face-up dominoes are doubles?
Count the shown dominoes that are doubles. {double_rule_text}
Looking at the visible dominoes, how many are doubles? {double_rule_text}
{double_rule_text} What is the number of face-up dominoes whose two halves match?
Among the shown dominoes, how many are doubles? {double_rule_text}

query:matching_end_count.connection_rule_text: A loose domino can connect to the open right end if either half has the same number of pips as that open end.

query:longest_chain_length_value.longest_chain_rule_text: Each loose domino may be used at most once. A domino can be added when either half matches the current open end. Do not count `REF`.

query:invalid_join_label.invalid_join_rule_text: A valid domino chain has matching pip numbers on the two halves that touch at each join.

query:higher_sum_than_reference_count.pip_sum_rule_text: The pip sum of a domino is the total number of pips across its two halves.

query:sum_to_target_count.pip_sum_rule_text: The pip sum of a domino is the total number of pips across its two halves.

query:double_count.double_rule_text: A double has the same number of pips on both halves.

code_prompt_defaults.object_description_chain_single_row: a short face-up domino chain across the top with one row of face-up candidate dominoes below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_chain_two_row: a short face-up domino chain across the top with two rows of face-up candidate dominoes below
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_chain_only: one face-up domino chain with six labeled joins
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_tableau_reference_sin: one row of face-up dominoes with one tile marked `REF`
code_prompt_defaults.object_description_tableau_reference_two: two rows of face-up dominoes with one tile marked `REF`
code_prompt_defaults.connection_rule_text: A loose domino can connect to the open right end if either half has the same number of pips as that open end.
code_prompt_defaults.longest_chain_rule_text: Each loose domino may be used at most once. A domino can be added when either half matches the current open end. Do not count `REF`.
code_prompt_defaults.invalid_join_rule_text: A valid domino chain has matching pip numbers on the two halves that touch at each join.
code_prompt_defaults.pip_sum_rule_text: The pip sum of a domino is the total number of pips across its two halves.
code_prompt_defaults.double_rule_text: A double has the same number of pips on both halves.
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_matching_end_count: set "annotation" to a JSON array of bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every loose domino that matches the `REF` tile's open right end
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_longest_chain_length_val: set "annotation" to a JSON array of bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for the loose dominoes in the unique longest chain from `REF`
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_invalid_join_label: set "annotation" to one segment using two pixel-point endpoints [x, y] in [[x0, y0], [x1, y1]] form, at the centers of the two touching domino halves at the invalid join
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_higher_sum_than_referenc: set "annotation" to a JSON array of bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every non-reference domino with a larger pip sum than `REF`
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_sum_to_target_count: set "annotation" to a JSON array of bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every shown domino with the target pip sum
code_prompt_defaults.annotation_hint_double_count: set "annotation" to a JSON array of bounding boxes [x0, y0, x1, y1] for every shown domino that is a double
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_matching_end_count: set "answer" to the count of loose dominoes that match the `REF` tile's open right end
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_longest_chain_length_value: set "answer" to the maximum number of loose dominoes that can be added after `REF`
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_invalid_join_label: set "answer" to the label of the invalid join, one of `A`, `B`, `C`, `D`, `E`, or `F`
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_higher_sum_than_reference_co: set "answer" to the count of loose dominoes with a larger pip sum than `REF`
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_sum_to_target_count: set "answer" to the count of shown dominoes with the target pip sum
code_prompt_defaults.answer_hint_double_count: set "answer" to the count of shown doubles
